corrections.note: Running head in scan has final lunate theta form; transcription uses standard theta. If character normalization is intended, ignore.
Transcript badly misread the word after κύκλῳ καὶ; scan reads καὶ τειχῶν ἐκ λίθων λαξευτῶν.

corrections.current: ἐπʼ Ἐκβατάνων καὶ κύκλῳ ἐλατρεύτην ἐκ λίθων λελαξευ-
corrections.correct: ἐπʼ Ἐκβατάνοις καὶ κύκλῳ ἐλατρεύτην ἐκ λίθων λαξευ-
corrections.note: City name is dative plural in body: Ἐκβατάνοις.

corrections.current: ἐπʼ Ἐκβατάνων καὶ κύκλῳ ἐλατρεύτην ἐκ λίθων λελαξευ-
corrections.correct: ἐπʼ Ἐκβατάνοις καὶ κύκλῳ ἐλατρεύτην ἐκ λίθων λαξευ-
corrections.note: Printed form is λαξευμένων, not λελαξευμένων; extra initial λε-.

corrections.current: καὶ πεδίᾳ Ἀριὼχ ὁ βασιλεὺς Ἐλυμαίων.
corrections.correct: καὶ πεδίῳ Ἀριὼχ ὁ βασιλεὺς Ἐλυμαίων.
corrections.note: Dative singular is πεδίῳ in scan.
Scan shows ℵc.a only here; trailing .b appears to have been carried over from following parenthesis.

corrections.current: om και 2° ℵc.a (hab ℵ* c.b) A
corrections.correct: om και 2° ℵc.a (hab ℵ* c.b) | A
corrections.note: There is a separator before A.
Verse number 3 begins a new apparatus item and is separated by a bar.
Scan appears to have a plus sign before A, indicating support for lemma reading.
Printed page number at foot is 781; user-supplied scan page is 798, but transcription should match printed page image. If page number is being transcribed, current already matches image.

uncertain.note: Final letterform in running head is lunate theta; unclear whether transcription policy distinguishes this from standard θ.

The phrase after κύκλῳ appears to read καὶ τειχῶν ἐκ λίθων λαξευμένων; existing transcription has ἐλατρεύτην, suggesting a larger corruption, but exact replacement span is difficult to anchor mechanically from the provided text.
At συνηντησεν] there may be a prefixed + before A; image is small.

notes: Body contains clear errors in Judith 1:2 and 1:6. Apparatus mostly matches, with a few separator/bar omissions and one clear letter misread. Printed page number is 781 on the image.
